title: pericopsis angolensis f. angolensis
intro: Pericopsis angolensis f. angolensis, also known as the African yellowwood, is a plant species within the family Lauraceae. Traditionally, the bark of this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits associated with Pericopsis angolensis f. angolensis include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments due to its antiseptic and analgesic properties. In herbal preparations, the bark is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are believed to promote wound healing and alleviate symptoms of various diseases.
